question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config{}] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition by flooding the Rosetta API with requests that exhaust Pool.MaxOpenConnections, causing all subsequent DB-backed network queries to block indefinitely and isolating the node from its data source?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config{}] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending concurrent requests that saturate Pool.MaxIdleConnections, forcing new connections to be created until MaxOpenConnections is hit, starving legitimate network-status queries of DB access?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config{}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a zero-value Pool.MaxLifetime to keep DB connections open indefinitely, causing connection leaks that eventually exhaust the pool and partition the node from its mirror database?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config{}] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that hold DB connections open longer than Pool.MaxLifetime, causing rapid connection churn that overwhelms the database and makes the node unreachable for network queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Http{}] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending a request with an extremely large body and holding the connection open, exploiting a zero or unset Http.ReadTimeout to tie up all server goroutines and prevent legitimate network participants from reaching the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Http{}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfigured Http.IdleTimeout of zero (infinite) to hold keep-alive connections open indefinitely, exhausting the server's connection table and causing a network partition by making the node unreachable to other peers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Http{}] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition by sending a slow-loris-style attack that exploits a large Http.ReadHeaderTimeout, keeping the server busy reading headers and preventing legitimate network-status requests from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Http{}] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that trigger slow response generation, exploiting a very large Http.WriteTimeout to hold server threads and block all outbound network communication from the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by triggering repeated address book refresh cycles through crafted API calls, exploiting a very short NodeRefreshInterval to flood the Hedera network with address book queries and cause the node to lose its routing table?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting a zero-value NodeRefreshInterval, which may cause continuous or unthrottled address book refresh loops that overwhelm the SDK client and disconnect the node from the Hedera network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting an extremely large NodeRefreshInterval (e.g., max time.Duration), causing the node to use a permanently stale address book that routes transactions to decommissioned nodes, effectively partitioning it from the live network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by observing that NodeMap is a map[string]hiero.AccountID with no size limit, and crafting a scenario where a very large NodeMap causes the SDK client to attempt connections to thousands of endpoints simultaneously, exhausting OS-level socket resources?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that NodeMap entries are not validated for reachability at config load time, allowing a deployment with all-unreachable node endpoints to silently route all transactions into a black hole, partitioning the node from the Hedera network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the absence of duplicate-key detection in NodeMap, where multiple entries mapping to the same hiero.AccountID but different endpoints cause the SDK to split transaction routing across conflicting targets, producing inconsistent network views?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config.Network] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by observing that the Network string field has no enum validation in the type definition, and that an invalid or empty network string causes the SDK client to fail silently or connect to the wrong network, partitioning the node from its intended peers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config.Network] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the Network field is lowercased at runtime but not validated against a known set, allowing a value like 'other' to cause the SDK to use an empty or undefined network topology, isolating the node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config.Online] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the boolean Online field, where a race condition or config reload causes the node to switch between online and offline modes mid-request, causing in-flight network queries to fail and producing a transient partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config.Online] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests to an Online=false node that expects only offline endpoints, causing the node to return errors for all network-status queries and making it appear partitioned from the network to monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config.ShutdownTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting a zero-value ShutdownTimeout, which causes the server to terminate all active connections immediately on shutdown signal, dropping in-flight network transactions and creating a partition window?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config.ShutdownTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending a flood of long-running requests just before a planned shutdown, exploiting a very short ShutdownTimeout that forces the server to kill connections before they complete, causing transaction loss and network inconsistency?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Config.ShutdownTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting an extremely large ShutdownTimeout, causing the server to hang indefinitely during shutdown while holding all network connections open, preventing the node from rejoining the network after a restart?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Db.StatementTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending queries that trigger long-running DB statements, exploiting a zero StatementTimeout to hold DB connections indefinitely and starve the network-status and block-retrieval endpoints of database access?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: Db.StatementTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending concurrent requests that each trigger a DB statement, exploiting a very large StatementTimeout to accumulate blocked connections until Pool.MaxOpenConnections is exhausted, making the node unable to serve network queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the hardcoded sslmode=disable in GetDsn(), performing a man-in-the-middle attack on the DB connection to inject false block data, causing the node to report an incorrect network state and diverge from the true chain?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting sslmode=disable in GetDsn() to intercept and drop DB responses, causing the node to receive empty or corrupted network topology data and route transactions to non-existent nodes?
